name: Rock Island Arsenal
tagline: The nation's largest government-owned arsenal and an Army command hub, on an island in the Mississippi between Illinois and Iowa.
_note: Unusual install: a manufacturing arsenal + command hub on a 946-acre island in the Mississippi, mostly civilian workforce, very limited on-post housing (almost everyone lives off post in the Quad Cities). Facts verified from official sources (home.army.mil/ria, rockislandhousing.com, DeCA/AAFES, Wikipedia for corroborated headline facts). BAH MHA IL089 (Rock Island) via the bah_rates single source of truth. Killer content is the Quad Cities IL-vs-IA decision. Medium-confidence flags: base coords (approx, Arsenal Island), representative off-base ZIP 61201 (Rock Island, IL).

data.Garrison: U.S. Army Garrison Rock Island Arsenal
data.Major commands: First Army HQ, Army Sustainment Command, Joint Munitions Command

sections.title: Mission & Identity
data: Rock Island Arsenal sits on a 946-acre island in the Mississippi River between Davenport, Iowa and Rock Island, Illinois. It is the nation's largest government-owned and operated arsenal, with the Joint Manufacturing and Technology Center producing metal components for all the armed services.

It is also a major command hub, home to First Army (the Army's oldest field army), the Army Sustainment Command, and the Joint Munitions Command. With 80-plus tenant organizations and a workforce that is mostly civilian, it functions more like a defense industrial campus than a typical post.

sections.title: Illinois or Iowa? The Quad Cities Decision

data.subtitle: The Arsenal sits on an island between two states, and with little on-post housing almost everyone lives off post. The first real choice is which side of the river, Illinois or Iowa.
intro: Because the Arsenal is a manufacturing and command installation with a mostly civilian workforce and very limited on-post housing, nearly everyone lives off post in the Quad Cities. That makes the metro, not the gate, the center of daily life.

The Quad Cities straddle the Mississippi: Rock Island, Moline, and East Moline on the Illinois side, and Davenport and Bettendorf on the Iowa side. The two states tax and school differently, so where you settle is partly a state-line decision, plus which bridge you will cross to the island each day.

recommendations.advice: The Iowa side, especially Bettendorf and the Pleasant Valley district, is well-regarded for schools; the Illinois side has solid options in Moline and Rock Island.
Rock Island and Moline sit closest to the Arsenal's gates; from the Iowa side you will cross a bridge to the island each day.
Illinois and Iowa tax income and property differently. Over a multi-year tour the difference adds up, so factor both into your budget.
With few military on post, lean on the Housing Services Office and local agents who know the Quad Cities rather than expecting a large on-base community.

sections.title: Gates & Access
data: The Arsenal is reached by bridges from both the Illinois and Iowa sides. Visitors without a DoD ID need a visitor pass and a REAL ID-compliant ID from the Visitor Control Center.
Gate hours and visitor requirements change, so confirm the current details on the official Rock Island Arsenal site before you go.

sections.title: On-Base Housing
data: On-post housing is limited (largely historic quarters), so the great majority of families live off post in the Quad Cities.
Start with the Arsenal's Housing Services Office, which can help with both the limited on-post options and finding a place off post on either side of the river.

source.label: DoD 2026 BAH Rates, MHA IL089 (Rock Island)

sections.title: Schools
data: There is no DoDEA district here, so children attend the local civilian public schools, and your district depends on which side of the river you choose. The Illinois side includes Rock Island-Milan and Moline-Coal Valley; the Iowa side includes Davenport, Bettendorf, and Pleasant Valley.
Your assigned schools depend on your address, so confirm the district and campus for any neighborhood you are considering.

data.Care: Primarily active-duty care on post; families and specialty care via TRICARE network providers in the Quad Cities

intro: Whether to rent or buy is the biggest money decision of most PCS moves, and the answer is mostly about how long you'll be here and how steady the local market is.
rent: Your tour is short (about two years or less) or your orders feel uncertain.
You want time to learn the area before committing to a neighborhood, or to a side of the river.
You'd rather not gamble on resale timing when you PCS out.
Your BAH comfortably covers rent in the areas you'd consider.
buy: You expect to be here three or more years.
You want to use your VA loan and build equity instead of paying rent.
The market is steady and you could rent the home out later if needed.
You've found a neighborhood and commute you're confident about.
data.bottom_line: There is no universal right answer. In the Quad Cities, factor in the Illinois-versus-Iowa tax differences and which bridge you'll cross. Run your BAH against the rent and home prices above, weigh your tour length, and talk to a lender and an agent who work with military families before you commit.

intro: The first month at a new duty station is mostly logistics. Knocking these out early makes everything else easier.

steps.title: Line up temporary lodging
steps.text: Book on-base lodging or a short-term rental before you arrive. You can claim Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) for a limited number of days while you house-hunt.

steps.title: In-process and update DEERS
steps.text: Report to your unit and finance, and make sure DEERS and ID cards are current for every family member so TRICARE and base access work.

steps.title: Start with the Housing Office
steps.text: Check on-base availability and get the housing office's read on off-base neighborhoods before you sign anything.

steps.title: Register your vehicle and sort access
steps.text: Handle base access and vehicle registration, and learn the visitor-pass process for guests. Bring your registration and insurance.

steps.title: Enroll the kids in school
steps.text: Contact the gaining school district or DoDEA early and bring records and immunizations. The School Liaison Office can help with the transfer.

steps.title: Set up healthcare
steps.text: Update your TRICARE region and enrollment so you can be seen at the local military treatment facility or network providers.

data.q: Is there housing on the Arsenal?
data.a: Very little. On-post housing is limited, so nearly all families live off post in the Quad Cities. Start with the Housing Services Office.

Both sides of the Mississippi are options. The Iowa side (Bettendorf, Pleasant Valley) is well-regarded for schools; the Illinois side (Rock Island, Moline) sits closest to the gates. The two states tax differently, so weigh that too.

data.q: What happens at Rock Island Arsenal?
data.a: It is the nation's largest government-owned arsenal and a command hub, home to First Army, Army Sustainment Command, Joint Munitions Command, and the Joint Manufacturing and Technology Center. The workforce is mostly civilian.
The Woodson Health Clinic on the Arsenal serves active-duty members; families and specialty care typically use TRICARE network providers in the Quad Cities. The region is TRICARE East.
Rock Island Arsenal is MHA IL089 (Rock Island). Use the calculator above for your grade.

picks.name: Rock Island Arsenal Museum
picks.blurb: On the island, one of the oldest U.S. Army museums, with firearms and Arsenal history.

picks.name: John Deere Pavilion
picks.blurb: A popular free attraction in downtown Moline showcasing John Deere equipment, past and present.

picks.name: Figge Art Museum
picks.blurb: A riverfront art museum in downtown Davenport on the Iowa side.

picks.name: Black Hawk State Historic Site
picks.blurb: A wooded historic site and trails along the Rock River in Rock Island.
